summary: CC-0110 turns the inert operators/c5c3 stub into a working ControlPlane orchestration operator for Keystone with K-ORC self-credentialing. The implementation is broad and largely high-quality: ControlPlane/CredentialRotation/SecretAggregate CRDs, a phased ControlPlaneReconciler (Infrastructure -> Keystone -> KORC -> AdminCredential -> Catalog) mirroring the keystone reference shape, dual defaulting+validation webhook, pure table-tested helpers (intervalToCron/projectPolicyOverrides), a complete Helm chart, CI wiring, deploy/ESO/OpenBao manifests, reference docs, and unit/envtest/Chainsaw tests. Verified directly in-sandbox: go vet clean; unit + envtest integration tests PASS (KUBEBUILDER_ASSETS=/usr/local/kubebuilder/bin/k8s/1.36.0-linux-amd64); make generate/manifests/sync-crds idempotent; make verify-crd-sync green; condition->metric drift guard and no-inline-literal guard PASS; REQ-013 credential-secrecy invariant enforced & tested (restricted:true, no workload leak); OpenBao narrow path added without widening the mid-glob; Helm ClusterRole RBAC matches the kubebuilder markers (secretaggregates read-only); servicemonitor defaults false; CI ALL_OPERATORS/FILTER_c5c3/paths-filter/Dockerfile-in-lint-dockerfiles all wired; SPDX present on all hand-authored files; architecture submodule untouched. HOWEVER, the K-ORC credential-bootstrap chain — the headline feature — contains two confirmed correctness defects that prevent a live cluster from ever bootstrapping, and the simulation-based tests mask both. These must be fixed before merge.

tests_checklist.item: Unit tests exist and pass (api, controller, metrics, main)
Envtest integration test exists and passes (TestIntegration_FullReconcile_ManagedToReady)
Condition->metric drift guard and no-inline-literal guard pass
Tests exercise the real credential bootstrap (not just a pre-populated Ready ExternalSecret)
Re-mint test asserts the full mutation cycle (new hash stamped)

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows the keystone reference reconciler shape (Owns/Watches, instrumentSubReconciler, early-return, condition aggregation)
Typed condition constants, no inline condition-type literals
Owner refs via controllerutil.SetControllerReference on every child
Helpers pure and immutability-safe; reuses ptr.To / shared conditions helpers
DECISION/Reviewer-please-verify notes resolved or backed by tests

security_checklist.item: Admin password / app-credential never mounted into service workloads (REQ-013)
Minted Application Credential is restricted:true (least privilege)
OpenBao write path matches policy; mid-path glob NOT widened; eso-management trailing glob covers read
No hardcoded secrets, no TLS-skip-verify, no secrets in cmdline args
SecretAggregate is types-only; chart RBAC grants only get/list/watch

architecture_checklist.item: Single CR kind per reconciler; CRD lifecycle by Helm not reconciler
Phased sub-reconcilers gated in dependency order with status conditions + ObservedGeneration
Watch handler matches actual ownership model (EnqueueRequestsFromMapFunc + field indexer for ESO-owned Secret)
Credential bootstrap chain composes end-to-end on a fresh cluster
K-ORC auth secret co-located with the resource CR that consumes it

dry_yagni_checklist.item: Reuses commonv1 types and internal/common helpers (secrets, conditions, bootstrap)
Missing-CRD safety and hash derivation centralized (no duplication)
Helm Role/ClusterRole share a single _helpers.tpl rbacRules partial (no rule drift)

fail_fast_checklist.item: Bad rotation interval surfaces a clean condition before partial apply
Missing CRD surfaces a condition (no crash-loop) via meta.IsNoMatchError
Status update failures preserved via errors.Join

defensive_checklist.item: Webhook defense-in-depth mirrors CRD markers (region, restricted, rotation.mode, secretName defaults; openStackRelease pattern; db/cache XOR; passwordSecretRef required)
PushSecret/owned-Secret writes are clobber-safe (mutate closure never touches K-ORC-owned .data)
Empty-value guard on openStackRelease validation (empty fails pattern, but no explicit 'required' message)

architecture_findings.description: The K-ORC admin-credential bootstrap chain cannot converge on a fresh cluster. K-ORC must authenticate (using k-orc-clouds-yaml) to mint the admin Application Credential, but k-orc-clouds-yaml is itself sourced from the minted credential's OpenBao path — with no admin-password-based bootstrap seed. There is no step that turns the root-of-trust admin password (kv-v2/bootstrap/keystone-admin) into an initial clouds.yaml K-ORC can use. Combined with the reconcileAdminCredential gate (see issues FC4/C1), the chain is circular and the AC never mints.
architecture_findings.fix: Introduce an explicit bootstrap seed for the K-ORC admin clouds.yaml derived from the admin password (e.g. a bootstrap ExternalSecret or a bootstrap step that writes a password-based clouds.yaml to openstack/keystone/admin/app-credential, marked ESO-managed so the operator's PushSecret can later overwrite it with the minted AC), and co-locate that Secret with the K-ORC resource CRs (see C1). Add a Chainsaw assertion that starts from an empty OpenBao AC path.

issues_found.description: Bootstrap circular dependency: reconcileAdminCredential GATES creation of the PushSecret (which writes openstack/keystone/admin/app-credential to OpenBao) on the k-orc-clouds-yaml ExternalSecret being Ready. But that ExternalSecret (deploy/eso/externalsecrets/k-orc-clouds-yaml.yaml) materializes FROM that exact OpenBao path. On a fresh cluster the path is empty, the ExternalSecret never becomes Ready, the function returns early before ever creating the PushSecret, so the path is never written -> permanent deadlock. write-bootstrap-secrets.sh seeds bootstrap/keystone-admin, infrastructure/mariadb and openstack/keystone/db but deliberately NOT openstack/keystone/admin/app-credential, so nothing breaks the cycle. Independently flagged by two review dimensions (confidence 0.95).
issues_found.fix: Do not gate the operator-owned-Secret/PushSecret creation on the consumer ExternalSecret it ultimately feeds. Reorder so the minted credential is pushed to OpenBao independently of clouds.yaml readiness, and/or seed openstack/keystone/admin/app-credential from the admin password at bootstrap (ESO-managed placeholder) so the ExternalSecret can become Ready before the first mint. Then re-add a readiness gate only on the downstream consumer if needed.
issues_found.evidence: reconcile_korc.go:388 `ready, err := secrets.WaitForExternalSecret(ctx, r.Client, types.NamespacedName{Namespace: korcCloudsYamlNamespace, Name: korcCloudsYamlSecretName})` ... :400 `if !ready { ... return ctrl.Result{RequeueAfter: korcRequeueAfter}, nil }` — returns BEFORE the PushSecret at :442-443 `ps := adminAppCredentialPushSecret(cp); secrets.EnsurePushSecret(...)`. k-orc-clouds-yaml.yaml:29 `key: openstack/keystone/admin/app-credential`. write-bootstrap-secrets.sh seeds only bootstrap/keystone-admin (:115), infrastructure/mariadb, openstack/keystone/db — not the AC path.

issues_found.description: Namespace mismatch for the K-ORC credential secret. The ApplicationCredential/Service/Endpoint CRs are created in childNamespace(cp)=cp.Namespace (production & E2E: 'openstack'), and the reconciler sets the REQUIRED Spec.CloudCredentialsRef.SecretName='k-orc-clouds-yaml'. Per K-ORC v2.5.0, CloudCredentialsRef is +required and GetCloudCredentialsRef() returns the resource's OWN namespace, i.e. K-ORC resolves 'k-orc-clouds-yaml' in 'openstack'. But the ESO ExternalSecret materializes 'k-orc-clouds-yaml' ONLY into 'orc-system'. K-ORC will not find the auth secret in 'openstack', so the AC never reaches Available, KORCReady stays False, and the whole chain stalls. (globalCloudConfig in the K-ORC HelmRelease does not save this: CloudCredentialsRef is required and per-resource, resolved in the resource namespace.)
issues_found.fix: Co-locate the auth clouds.yaml Secret with the K-ORC resource CRs: either materialize 'k-orc-clouds-yaml' into the ControlPlane namespace (openstack) as well, or create the K-ORC ApplicationCredential/Service/Endpoint in orc-system, or set CloudCredentialsRef to a secret that exists in childNamespace. Verify on a live cluster.
issues_found.evidence: K-ORC v2.5.0 zz_generated.applicationcredential-resource.go:79-80 `// +required` `CloudCredentialsRef CloudCredentialsReference`; credentials_ref.go:28 'secretName is the name of a secret in the same namespace as the resource being provisioned'; GetCloudCredentialsRef() returns `&i.Namespace, &i.Spec.CloudCredentialsRef`. reconcile_korc.go:151 `Namespace: childNamespace(cp)` (=cp.Namespace per reconcile_infrastructure.go:41-43); :157-160 sets `CloudCredentialsRef.SecretName`. k-orc-clouds-yaml.yaml:17 `namespace: orc-system`.

issues_found.description: The unit and integration tests mask the bootstrap defects above by pre-populating the k-orc-clouds-yaml ExternalSecret with Ready=True and pre-syncing it before creating the ControlPlane. As a result the suite passes while the live chain deadlocks. There is no fresh-cluster bootstrap test that starts from an empty OpenBao AC path / absent clouds.yaml.
issues_found.location: operators/c5c3/internal/controller/reconcile_korc_test.go (readyCloudsYamlES); operators/c5c3/internal/controller/integration_test.go (pre-sync of the clouds.yaml ExternalSecret before CR creation)
issues_found.fix: Add a test (envtest or Chainsaw) that does NOT pre-populate k-orc-clouds-yaml as Ready and instead drives the real bootstrap ordering, so the circular dependency surfaces as a failing test rather than being hidden.
issues_found.evidence: reconcile_korc_test.go defines readyCloudsYamlES() returning an ExternalSecret with Ready=True status; reconcileAdminCredential tests and integration_test.go rely on it so reconcileAdminCredential passes its gate. Without it the gate at reconcile_korc.go:400 would never clear.

issues_found.description: Tautological / language-semantics-only tests that exercise no production logic, contradicting the project's own reject-tautological-constant-tests and remove-tests-that-only-verify-language-semantics patterns. TestUpdatePhaseConstants/TestRotationModeConstants assert string(UpdatePhaseIdle)=='Idle' (constant equals its own literal); TestControlPlaneSpecZeroValues/TestControlPlaneStatusZeroValues assert uninitialized fields equal Go zero values; TestControlPlaneImplementsRuntimeObject is a compile-time blank-identifier assertion dressed as a test.
issues_found.fix: Delete these tests; they add coverage noise without catching any behavior. Keep only tests that exercise webhook/reconciler logic.
issues_found.evidence: controlplane_status_test.go TestUpdatePhaseConstants/TestRotationModeConstants assert enum string == literal; controlplane_types_test.go TestControlPlaneSpecZeroValues asserts zero values; TestControlPlaneImplementsRuntimeObject uses `var _ runtime.Object = &ControlPlane{}`.

issues_found.description: No aggregate webhook validation test. The webhook accumulates all field errors (no short-circuit), but there is no single test (e.g. RunsAllValidations) that puts every rule into a broken state simultaneously and asserts every error substring is present (openStackRelease pattern, database XOR, cache XOR, passwordSecretRef required). Per the project's update-aggregate-validation-tests / ensure-test-names-accurately-describe-coverage patterns this should exist so a future short-circuit regression is caught.
issues_found.fix: Add TestValidateCreate_RunsAllValidations that violates all four rules at once and asserts the returned aggregate error contains each field-specific substring.
issues_found.evidence: controlplane_webhook.go validate() appends to allErrs for all four checks (no early return), but controlplane_webhook_test.go only has isolated single-rule tests.

issues_found.description: Re-mint and no-op tests verify only half the mutation cycle. The re-mint test asserts the admin-password-hash annotation is cleared, but never drives the subsequent reconcile to assert the NEW hash is stamped and the AC re-minted. The hash-match no-op test asserts the annotation is unchanged but does not assert the ApplicationCredential spec is not otherwise mutated (no spurious update). Per assert-absence-of-old-state / full-mutation-cycle review patterns.
issues_found.fix: Extend the re-mint test to run a second reconcile and assert the fresh hash is stamped; extend the no-op test to assert AC.Spec is unchanged (no churn).
issues_found.evidence: reconcile_credentialrotation_test.go TestRotation_ReMintClearsHashAnnotation asserts cleared annotation only; TestRotation_HashMatchIsNoOp asserts annotation unchanged only.

issues_found.description: The envtest integration test drives the full chain (Infra -> Keystone -> KORC -> AdminCredential -> Catalog -> Ready=True) but only asserts the final aggregate condition and observedGeneration. It does not assert the intermediate projected specs (Keystone image tag derived from openStackRelease, clusterRefs wired, merged policy, AC restricted/Unrestricted inversion, Service/Endpoint shape), so a projection regression would not be caught by the integration tier.
issues_found.fix: Add intermediate assertions on the projected child specs at each phase boundary, not just the final Ready=True.
issues_found.evidence: integration_test.go TestIntegration_FullReconcile_ManagedToReady asserts only the terminal ControlPlane Ready condition and sub-conditions; no Get+assert on the projected Keystone.Spec.Image.Tag, Database.ClusterRef, PolicyOverrides, or AC Unrestricted.

issues_found.description: The image-tag projection test asserts the exact value (Tag=='2025.2') for a single openStackRelease but does not prove the derivation rule (a different release yields a different tag). A second table row would lock in the derivation rather than a coincidental literal match.
issues_found.fix: Make it table-driven with at least two releases (e.g. 2025.2 and 2026.1) asserting Tag==release each time, plus one image-override case.
issues_found.evidence: reconcile_keystone.go:74 `Tag: cp.Spec.OpenStackRelease`; test asserts a single hardcoded '2025.2'.

issues_found.description: Several generated CRD field descriptions are truncated mid-sentence because a doc-comment sentence flows directly into a +kubebuilder marker line, which controller-gen treats as a marker (not description). e.g. rotation 'mode' renders 'Mode selects the rotation strategy. Defaults to PasswordDriven via BOTH the' then stops. Affects restricted, rotation.mode, and region descriptions; degrades `kubectl explain`.
issues_found.fix: Reword so the marker is not mid-sentence: end the human-readable sentence before the '// +kubebuilder:default=...' line (e.g. put the 'via BOTH the marker AND webhook' note on a line that precedes the marker, or drop it from the doc comment).
issues_found.evidence: c5c3.io_controlplanes.yaml:302-307 `default: PasswordDriven` / `description: Mode selects the rotation strategy. Defaults to PasswordDriven via BOTH the` / `enum:` — sentence cut at 'the'.

issues_found.description: The scheduled-rotation deferral DECISION comment in credentialrotation_types.go does not cite the requirement ID (REQ-015), unlike the matching DECISION in reconcile_credentialrotation.go. Minor traceability gap. (Note: the deferral itself IS explicitly called out — DECISION comment + a 'ScheduledRotationDeferred' event — satisfying REQ-015's 'not silently dropped'; the PR description should also state it.)
issues_found.fix: Add the (CC-0110, REQ-015) tag to the deferral DECISION comment, and ensure the PR description explicitly notes the scheduled loop is deferred.
issues_found.evidence: credentialrotation_types.go:67-73 documents the deferred intervalDays/preRotationDays/gracePeriodDays without a REQ-015 reference; reconcile_credentialrotation.go:96 references it.

issues_found.description: The architecture/ submodule pointer was not bumped and the PR (as represented in the worktree) carries no link to the upstream architecture commit/PR for 08-c5c3-operator.md / 05-korc.md / 01-credential-lifecycle.md. The acceptance criteria require the pointer bumped OR an upstream link. Submodule files are correctly NOT modified (read-only), so this is a process/traceability item, not a code defect.
issues_found.fix: Bump the architecture submodule pointer once the upstream docs land, or add the upstream commit/PR link to the PR description.
issues_found.evidence: git diff main...HEAD shows no architecture/ entry and no submodule pointer change; git submodule status shows architecture at e3ba3b0 (heads/main), unchanged.

suggested_improvements: Resolve the four 'Reviewer: please verify' K-ORC integration decisions in reconcile_korc.go with live-cluster evidence: adminUserRef convention (CloudName -> sibling User CR), keystoneEndpointURL (http://keystone.<ns>.svc:5000/v3), AccessRule.Service -> serviceRef naming, and the PushSecret DeletionPolicy=None choice. These sit on the same credential path as the two confirmed defects.

Consider validating spec.services.keystone.rotationInterval in the webhook (only 168h or multiples of 24h are accepted by intervalToCron) so a bad interval is rejected at admission instead of surfacing as a steady-state KeystoneReady=False with no requeue.
intervalToCron maps any multiple of 24h (e.g. 48h, 336h) to a DAILY schedule, silently losing fidelity for non-weekly multi-day intervals; document this or reject non-{24h,168h} intervals explicitly.

Set the aggregate Ready=False condition on early-return paths too (currently setReadyCondition runs only after all five sub-reconcilers pass), so Status.Ready reflects in-progress state rather than remaining absent until full convergence.
next_steps: FIX BEFORE MERGE (BLOCKER): break the K-ORC clouds.yaml bootstrap cycle (FC4) and co-locate the auth Secret with the K-ORC resource CRs (C1). Add an admin-password-derived bootstrap seed for openstack/keystone/admin/app-credential.

Add a non-masking bootstrap test (TE9) that starts from an empty OpenBao AC path so the circular dependency would fail the suite; validate the full chain on a real kind cluster via the Chainsaw suite.

Remove the tautological/language-semantics tests (TE_TAUTOLOGY) and add the aggregate webhook validation test (TE3); complete the re-mint mutation-cycle assertions (TE7) and integration intermediate-spec assertions (TE7b).
Address the MINOR items: CRD description truncation (D1), REQ-015 traceability (FEATURE_ID), image-tag derivation table test (PR1), and the architecture submodule pointer/upstream link (DA3).

detected_patterns.name: Missing-CRD safety via meta.IsNoMatchError
detected_patterns.applies_when: A sub-reconciler create-or-updates a CR whose CRD is owned by a third-party operator that may not be installed yet (e.g. K-ORC ApplicationCredential/Service/Endpoint)
detected_patterns.description: When create-or-update of a third-party CR fails, the error is checked with meta.IsNoMatchError. On a no-match (CRD absent) the sub-reconciler logs, sets its *Ready condition to False with a stable Reason ('KORCCRDNotInstalled') and a short RequeueAfter, and returns nil — so a missing optional CRD degrades to a clear condition instead of crash-looping the operator. Used consistently across the K-ORC mint and catalog phases; future operators that own optional third-party CRDs should follow the same shape.
